observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to
conduct physical count of PPE taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed
in COA Circular no. 2020-006, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
refile the request for write-off of the dormant receivables and attached the relevant
supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of
the Audit Team since its collectability is remote.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
verify with the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of P452,961.81 and
P48,281.97 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in
the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of
the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.

observations.managementAction:
On-going Completion of the inventory.
PAR is now being issued.
Maintenance of PPELC;
On-going physical count.
On-going physical count.
observations.reason:
Completion of the annual physical count was not made.


The reconciliation could be made once the annual physical count has been completed.
Completion of the annual physical count was not made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Land account, particularly Transfer the cost of the lot that was dropped from the books of account which pertains to the amount of the lot that are not yet donated to the PNP.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant regarding the unrecorded parcels of land for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements; and
b. facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and the immediate titling of the lots once ownership is determined.
observations.managementAction:
Deed of Donation was executed between the Municipality and PNP.
The Municipal Accountant recorded the two unrecorded parcels of land.
Ongoing process of titling.
observations.reason: Titling of the eco-park in Barangay Mabini Ibaba is still in process; the owner of the lot in Pajarillo will buy back the lot since the area is not good to be a dumpsite while the tax declaration of the remaining two parcels will be facilitated once the final report from the Geodetic Engineer is received.

observations.managementAction:
Issuance of RIS and SSMI is now being implemented.
Office Supplies is now procured in the DBM-PS.
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to submit regularly to Property Custodian the RIS forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) including elected local officials and department heads undergo capacity building programs on GAD which shall include but not be limited to gender mainstreaming, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and GAD-related tools for the GFPS to be fully functional and to ensure that GAD PPAs were supported by HGDG tools.
observations.managementAction: The schedule of training is set.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to ensure the immediate implementation of development projects, as well as its full utilization, so that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Projects amounting to P31,865,923.41 were implemented this year. Also, the six continuing projects with a total appropriation of P15,766,619.76 were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Officer (MDRRMO) to carefully study the feasibility of the projects before inclusion in the LDRRMP;
b. MDRRMO to facilitate the immediate implementation and completion of programs/projects/activities (PPAs) of Disaster Risk and Reduction Fund (DRRMF) within the budget year in order to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity and hence reduce its risk; and
c. Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Council (LDRRMC) to regularly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Plan (LDRRMP).
observations.managementAction: Projects amounting to P98,370.00 and P2,225,000.00 under current and continuing appropriation, respectively, were implemented.
observations.reason: Current Appropriation for MF and Continuing Appropriation totaling P4,307,995.92 and P6,627,378.56, respectively, or with a total amount of P10,935,374.48 were not implemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and other members of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) expedite the:
- preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council and to the Environment and Management Bureau (EMB) to ensure that programs/projects for SWM are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework (NSWMF);
- process the documentary requirements regarding the construction of sanitary landfill; and
- intensify information campaign and education in all barangays on the necessity of MRF, to ensure protection of public health and environment.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide assistance for the establishment of MRF in six barangays.
observations.managementAction:
The preparation of the SWMP has been drafted.
The application for construction of the sanitary landfill is for approval by the EMB.
Almost all the barangays have MRFs.
Five additional MRFs were established.
observations.reason:
The application for construction of the sanitary landfill is for approval by the EMB.
Construction of the sanitary landfill is still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a final written notice to the contractor, otherwise, take legal actions to compel the contractor to refund the unrecouped advance payment of P7,384,897.05.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects; 
b. forfeit the performance security posted by the contractor; and
c. impose appropriate sanctions on the consultant for the original detailed engineering design which failed to consider the conditions that led to the need for additional work costing more than 10 percent of the original total contract price.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality demanded the contractor to refund the unrecouped amount.
Demand Letter was already sent to the Contractor.
Blacklisting Order was issued.
Performance security was forfeited.
Appropriate sanctions were imposed on the consultant.
observations.reason: Demand Letter was already sent to the Contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) including elected local officials and department heads undergo capacity building programs on GAD which shall include but not be limited to gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and GAD-related tools for the GFPS to be fully functional.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GFPS Focal Person to coordinate the duly approved/endorsed GPB to all concerned municipal officials to ensure inclusion of GAD PPAs in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: Office Order No. 96-2021 dated March 10, 2021 was issued for the designation of new MGAD Focal Point Person. The new designated MGAD Focal Person is still in the process of learning and familiarizing the Gender Mainstreaming Monitoring System (GMMS) Version 3. The GAD Focal Person approached the assigned MLGOO and DILG Staff/Officer for their technical assistance and was guided on how the system works. HGDG tools were not utilized for some offices since the collection of sex-disaggregated data, gender-related information and gender analysis is not updated. The GAD Focal Person is continuously working on the GPB Online Monitoring System to update the comments and recommendations of the reviewer. References to utilize the HGDG guidelines were collected to prepare GAD Plan and Reports. All COA comments and recommendations are acknowledged and will be worked on to avoid recurrence of these findings.
observations.reason: Some details were not encoded and visible in the GPB and AR because the GAD Focal Person was not the one who attended the Seminar/Orientation on GAD Encoding of GPB in the Monitoring System; HGDG tools were not utilized for some offices since sex-disaggregated data, gender-related information and gender analysis are not updated; need for a refresher course, review and coaching on proper administration of GAD Checklist and its Guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer to:
a. employ necessary actions to address the noted causes of delay in the implementation of projects pertaining to CYs 2018 and 2019;
b. study carefully the feasibility of the projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP); and
c. strictly follow the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) particularly the timetable for the implementation of projects to ensure timely completion of development projects so that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to enhance the monitoring of implementation of development projects and to regularly submit a status report to the Office of the Municipal Mayor for immediate resolution of related problems, if any.
observations.managementAction: Considering that 20 projects continued in CY 2020 with total continuing appropriation of P37,292,212.55, 11 projects with allocated funds totaling P28,700,050.29 were either partially or not implemented as of December 31, 2020. The Municipal Engineer, who is also the OIC-MPDC together with the Local Finance Committee were instructed to carefully study the feasibility and the benefits to be derived from the project before inclusion in the 20 percent Annual Investment Plan (AIP). The Municipal Engineer was also instructed to strictly monitor the timeline for the implementation of each PPA to ensure that the programs and projects will be delivered and completed on time. Of the unimplemented projects with continuing appropriation, one was already implemented, one was on-going while the remaining eight were reprogrammed to other projects. Of the 11 projects with current appropriation, four were implemented, one was on-going, one is for implementation while the remaining five were also reprogrammed.
observations.reason: Monitoring and submission of status report was not consistently done. Necessary actions to address the causes of delay were not fully employed; some projects were reprogrammed; overall utilization for CY 2020 was only 38.10 percent or P12,000,000.00 out of P31,493,215.87.

observations.managementAction: Included in the unutilized LDRRMF is a project amounting to P2,500,000.00 which was bidded but was not awarded; however, the same was implemented in CY 2021. The unutilized current appropriation amount was transferred to the Trust Fund. All funds under TF were re-planned by the LDRRM Council. Directives have been conveyed to the MDRRMO to evaluate the PPAs before inclusion in the LDRRMP. Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM is now being submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: PPAs with appropriation for Mitigation Fund and Continuing Appropriation were not implemented. Some projects were bidded but not awarded; re-planning was necessary; implementation and regular monitoring had been weak.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct the actual physical count of the Municipality’s
property and prepare the RPCPPE;
b. Property Custodian-Designate to ensure that all PPE counted by the Inventory
Committee are issued with PAR and provided with PC which should be
regularly updated;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELC; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian-Designate to reconcile the
accounting and property records.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor constantly monitor the status
of physical inventory taking to ensure completion and submission of complete
RPCPPE within the deadline.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
adjust the Land account, particularly the cost of the lot that was dropped from the books of
account which pertains to the amount of the lot that are not yet donated to the Philippine
National Police (PNP).

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Assessor to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant regarding the unrecorded parcels of
land for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements; and
b. facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and the immediate titling of the lots
once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third party
claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian-Designate to strictly comply with
the regulations on proper accounting and management of all supplies and
materials to have a reliable balance of inventories and related expenses
accounts and to safeguard the same against possible misappropriation;
b. Property Custodian-Designate and all concerned department/office heads to
ensure a centralized procurement of office supplies which, as much as
possible, should be done through the DBM-PS; and
c. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to
submit regularly to the Property Custodian-Designate the SSMI pertaining to
the consumed accountable forms, medical, dental and laboratory supplies and
agricultural supplies, respectively, to be recognized as an expense.

observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Not Implemented
Not Implemented
Not Implemented
Not Implemented
observations.reason:
RPCPPE was not submitted due to voluminous work in Municipal General Services Office.
PAR and PC were incomplete and not updated due to workload.
No action was taken by management, hence, PPELCs were still not maintained.
No action was taken by management, hence, reconciliation was not made.
RPCPPE was not submitted due to voluminous work in the Municipal General Services Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to obtain from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the list of individual taxpayers to facilitate preparation of subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer in order to facilitate the preparation of reports/schedules at any given time;
b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the list of taxpayers who availed the Compromise Agreement as of December 31, 2020 and the report on the status of compliance of each taxpayer with the Agreement for evaluation; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to submit a request to the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of collection remedies provided in RA No. 7160, supported with complete list of delinquent taxpayers and statement of reason/s for non-collection or non-payment by taxpayers despite the Compromise Agreement, in order for the Municipality to have additional funds for other development projects that will benefit its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other regarding the parcels of land with no tax declaration in the name of the Municipality and the unrecorded parcels in the books in order to establish which parcels of land should be recorded or dropped from the books for fair presentation of the Land account; and
b. OIC-Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and the immediate titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted a copy of Tax Declaration No. 18-0018-00718 for the Road Lot located at Olongtao Ibaba with a recorded amount of P91,350.00. The unrecorded parcels in the books were already submitted and donated in order to establish which parcels of land should be recorded or dropped from the books for fair presentation of the Land account. The Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined.
observations.reason: Various parcels have still no Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and/or Tax Declaration; No tax declaration and TCT was gathered/processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO and BAC to always coordinate regarding the awarded procurement contracts to ensure recording in the RAAO of all obligations incurred during the year.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the list of awarded contracts. Awarded contracts are now being recorded in the RAAO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian-Designate to strictly comply with the regulations on proper accounting and management of all supplies and materials in order to have reliable balances of inventories and related expenses accounts and to safeguard the same against possible misappropriation;
b. Property Custodian-Designate and all concerned department/office heads to ensure a centralized procurement of office supplies which, as much as possible, should be done through the Department of Budget and Management-Procurement Service (DBM-PS); and
c. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to submit regularly to the Property Custodian-Designate the SSMI pertaining to the consumed accountable forms, medical, dental and laboratory supplies and agricultural supplies, respectively, for recording in the SC.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the RPCI for the office supplies. The Municipal Accountant used the physical count conducted as reference to adjust the balance of the account. The Municipal Treasurer counted the items remaining in her possession to adjust Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers. Property Custodian and all other department heads were instructed to adhere to the guidelines of Supplies and Property Management particularly the maintenance of complete ICS and the regular submission of SSMI and other needed reports.
observations.reason: No RPCI for Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers and SSMI was still not prepared.

observations.managementAction: Submitted the documents required by the Audit Team for evaluation; BAC Chairman to submit the documents. Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to require the contractor to submit surety bond. The contractor already made a refund of liquidated damages totaling P95,141.05 (For issuance of Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance). The Municipal Engineer submitted explanation and is closely monitoring the project implementation. BAC Chairman ensured documentary requirements. Municipal Accountant reviewed the surety bond. Liquidated damages were imposed and deducted from Disbursement Vouchers. The Municipal Engineer is monitoring the project implementation. DVs have signatures of the payee.
observations.reason: Details and required documents were submitted. Some items required issuance of Notices (e.g. Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance). The Municipal Mayor gave instructions to the officials concerned with compliance with the rules and regulations provided in RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman of Committee on Inventory/Appraisal and Property Custodian-Designate to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Audit Team for verification and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations to avoid further deterioration in order to earn at least the salvage value of these assets and to unclog the very limited office space/storage of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The unserviceable properties were already disposed of.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections promptly and intact to avoid the risk of loss and/or misuse of which the MT complied.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustment for the under recorded collection of P90.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer deposited intact and promptly all her collections to comply with the requirement. Adjusted under JEV Number 100-2020-06-007-43 dated June 25, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of every year the list of all on-going government PPAs of the immediately preceding year;
b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted to facilitate validation; and
c. submit to the Audit Team the monthly Monitoring Report on the status of implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
List of all on-going government PPAs was submitted to the Audit Team.
The Audit Team was informed of the posting of signboards within the period, together with the pictures of the signboards posted.
Monthly Monitoring Report on the status of implementation of PPAs was submitted regularly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MENRO-Designate and all other members of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) expedite the:
• preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council and to the EMB to ensure that programs/projects for SWM are consistent with the NSWMF;
• process the documentary requirements regarding the construction of sanitary landfill; and
• intensify information campaign and education in all barangays on the necessity of MRF, to ensure protection of public health and environment.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide assistance for the establishment of MRF in six barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has re-launched their SWM Program for the 10-year SWMP. The Municipality adopted the old version of the SWMP; addendum regarding the disposal of special waste is on-going. For the construction of a sanitary landfill, the Municipality is in the process of data gathering for the online application of the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC). The Punong Barangay of the six Barangays were encouraged to fabricate their MRFs for the proper implementation of waste segregation.
observations.reason: The revised 10-Year SWMP was still not completed (only three chapters finished). Sanitary landfill was not yet constructed. Six barangays still have no MRF.

observations.managementAction: Termination of contract was implemented on November 18, 2019. The Municipality demanded the contractor to refund the remaining unrecouped advance payment of P7,384,897.05. The Municipality informed the consultant of the technical shortfall on the original detailed engineering design. As for the issuance of the Blacklisting Order, this was not done yet in the hope that they may find a solution that is a "win-win" to everyone. Performance security was still not forfeited in favor of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Advance payment of P7,384,897.05 was still not refunded by the contractor. Performance security was still not forfeited in favor of the Municipality. Issuance of a Blacklisting Order was not done. Appropriate sanctions on the consultant were not imposed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Assessment Operations Officer to facilitate the immediate titling of the subject lots in the name of the Municipality, to avoid possible third party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 3, Page 35.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing regulation.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: No report submitted to the Audit Team during CY 2019. Only one report pertaining to completed projects was submitted in January 2020. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 9, Page 53.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of separate budget of programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided in accordance with RA No. 9710 or MCW and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: Programs/Projects/Activities attributed to GAD budget could not be specifically identified from CY 2019 Annual Investment Program. Only the utilization of P500,000.00 was ascertained. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 10, Page 55.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct the actual physical count of the
Municipality’s property and prepare the RPCPPE;
b. Property Custodian-Designate to ensure that all PPE counted by the
Inventory Committee are issued with PAR and provided with PC which
should be regularly updated;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELC; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian-Designate to reconcile the
accounting and property records.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor constantly monitor the
status of physical inventory taking to ensure completion and submission of
complete RPCPPE within the deadline.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to obtain from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the list
of individual taxpayers with delinquencies for CY 2001 and below and
another list for CYs 2002 to 2019 as of December 31, 2019 to serve as her
basis for preparing the subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer in order to
facilitate preparation of necessary reports/schedules at any given time;
b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the list of taxpayers who
availed of the Compromise Agreement and the report on the status of
compliance of each taxpayer with the Agreement for evaluation; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to submit a request to the Sangguniang Bayan for
assistance in the enforcement of collection remedies provided in RA No.
7160, supported with complete list of delinquent taxpayers and statement of
reason/s for non-collection or non-payment by taxpayers despite the
Compromise Agreement, in order for the Municipality to have additional
funds for other development projects that will benefit its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to coordinate regarding
the parcels of land with no tax declaration in the name of the Municipality,
unrecorded in the books and already donated in order to establish which
parcels of land should be recorded or dropped from the books for fair
presentation of Land account balance; and
b. OIC-Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and
the immediate titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is
determined to avoid possible third party claims and incurrence of litigation
costs in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO and BAC to always
coordinate regarding the awarded procurement contracts to ensure recording in
the RAAO of all obligations incurred during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian-Designate to strictly comply
the regulations on proper accounting and management of all supplies and
materials in order to have reliable balances of inventories and related
expenses accounts and to safeguard the same against possible
misappropriation;
b. Property Custodian-Designate and all concerned department/office heads to
ensure a centralized procurement of office supplies which, as much as
possible, should be done through the DBM-PS; and
c. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to
submit regularly to the Property Custodian-Designate the SSMI and RIS
pertaining to the consumed accountable forms, medical, dental and
laboratory supplies and agricultural supplies, respectively, for recording in
the SC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the BAC Chairman to submit the
lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation;
b. Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to require the Contractor of the
project Construction of Olongtao Ibaba-San Nicholas-San Vicente FMR to
submit a copy of surety bond with indicated effectivity date duly certified by
the issuing insurance company;
c. Municipal Accountant to require the concerned contractors to refund the
amount of liquidated damages totaling P95,141.05;
d. Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation regarding the three
projects which contracts were not terminated despite incurrence of negative
slippage ranging from 48.11 percent to 49.79 percent as of expiry date of
contract;
e. BAC Chairman to ensure strict compliance with the documentary
requirements to substantiate the procurement activities of the Municipality;
f. Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to ensure that the surety bond
submitted as warranty bond is valid for contracts with local government
units and the effectivity date and duration of the bond are properly indicated
therein;
g. Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidated damages in future
transactions, if any, are duly recognized and deducted before payments are
made to contractors on the account of its procurement activities;
h. Municipal Engineer to closely monitor/supervise the actual accomplishments
of Municipal projects to ensure that they are performed and completed as
scheduled; and
i. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the payee acknowledged the payment by
affixing his signature on the DV before the release of check.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate compliance with the
IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the period of action on procurement activities and
termination of contract when the project incurred negative slippage.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman of Committee
on Inventory/Appraisal and Property Custodian-Designate to prepare and
submit the IIRUP to the Audit Team for verification and to undertake the
necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with
applicable rules and regulations to avoid further deterioration in order to earn at
least the salvage value of these assets and to unclog the very limited office
space/storage of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit her collections promptly and intact to avoid the risk of loss and/or
misuse of undeposited collections.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustment for the
under recorded collection of P90.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned implementing offices to:
a. submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of every year the list of all on-
going government PPAs of the immediately preceding year;
b. inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project or before
the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards
and/or public notices are already posted to facilitate validation; and
c. submit to the Audit Team the monthly monitoring report on the status of
implementation of PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all members of the
GFPS including elected local officials and department heads undergo capacity
building programs on GAD which shall include but not be limited to gender
mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting,
and GAD-related tools in order for the GFPS to be fully functional.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GFPS Focal
Person to coordinate the duly approved/endorsed GPB to all concerned
municipal officials to ensure inclusion of GAD PPAs in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC and the Municipal
Engineer to:
a. employ necessary actions to address the noted causes of delay in the
implementation of projects pertaining to CYs 2018 and 2019;
b. study carefully the feasibility of the projects before inclusion in the Annual
Investment Plan (AIP); and
c. strictly follow the Project Procurement Management Plan particularly the
timetable for the implementation of development projects to ensure timely
completion so that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that
could be derived therefrom.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to
enhance the monitoring of implementation of development projects and to
regularly submit a status report to the Office of the Municipal Mayor for
immediate resolution of related problems, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the projects before inclusion in
the LDRRMP;
b. MDRRMO to facilitate the immediate implementation and completion of the
crucial programs/projects/activities (PPAs) of DRRMF within the budget year
in order to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity and
hence reduce its risk;
c. MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month through the LDRRM Council (LDRRMC) and the LDC in compliance
with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
d. MDRRMO and LDRRMC to prepare the supplemental LDRRMP for CY 2020
for the total amount of P5,552,378.86 continued to CY 2020 which is allegedly
for re-planning; and
e. LDRRMC to regularly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the
LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MENRO-Designate and all other
members of MESWMB expedite the preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP
and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council and to
EMB to ensure that programs/projects for SWM are consistent with the
NSWMF; implement the construction of sanitary landfill; provide assistance for
the establishment of MRF in six barangays; and intensify information campaign
and education in all barangays on the necessity of MRF, to ensure protection of
public health and environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. implement at once the termination of contract of this Project;
b. require the contractor to refund the remaining unrecouped advance payment of
P7,384,897.05;
c. issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from
participating in the bidding of all government projects;
d. forfeit the performance security posted by the contractor;
e. impose appropriate sanctions on the consultant for the original detailed
engineering design which failed to consider the conditions that led to the
need for additional work costing more than 10 percent of the original total
contract price; and
f. initiate strict compliance with IMA regarding the required minimum
accomplishment for the release of payments.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPMIU to submit
to the Audit Team a report on the updated status of the Project and the possible
remedies for its continuance, for evaluation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort and adopting the remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Sent notices to delinquent taxpayers. The Municipality also continues to support the project launched by the Provincial Government of Quezon authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to enter into a Compromise Agreement.
observations.reason: Only 13.36 percent of RPT receivables was collected in CY 2019. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 2, Page 33.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Property Custodian to prepare and submit the IIRUP and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the IIRUP as of October 31, 2019 for unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment.
observations.reason: Unserviceable properties totaling P92,351.30 still has no IIRUP. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 7, Page 49.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Various development projects implemented. Ten out of 22 development projects were continued from CYs 2017 and 2018 and were still not implemented during CY 2019.
observations.reason: Ten out of 22 development projects continued from CYs 2017 and 2018 were still not implemented during CY 2019. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 11, Page 58.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Property Custodian to prepare and submit the IIRUP, and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) has already created a disposal committee and has been advised to re-inspect the unserviceable property, conduct appraisal and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP). Timeliness has been set for the completion of the report and the conduct of disposal.
observations.reason: Difficulty in conducting the inspection and assessing the scrap value of each property. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 29)

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort and adopt the remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer exerted utmost effort to collect delinquent real property taxes thru issuance of warrant of levy and public advertisement for sale on auction to enforce collection in accordance with RA No. 7160. Further, the Municipality continues to support the project launched by the Provincial Government of Quezon authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to enter into a Compromise Agreement where delinquent taxpayers are given option to pay tax due on an installment basis.
observations.reason: Unknown whereabouts of some taxpayers and unavailability of contact information. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, Page 28)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the immediate titling of the subject lots to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: One lot has been donated to the local water district since subject lot is the location of the reservoir. Another one for donation to Olongtao National High School since it is the location of the schools oval. One lot already in process and the one is still subject for deliberation if will be donated to PNP since it is the location of military camp and the other one still pending deliberation if it has to be sold or subject for an exchange.
observations.reason: Unavailability of documents to facilitate the transfer of title. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 27)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator has been instructed to facilitate timely implementation of the LGU’s programs and projects to ensure that maximum benefits from such projects can be obtained.
observations.reason: 20 percent Development Fund was still not fully utilized in CY 2018. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, Page No. 31)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to appropriate at least 1 percent of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer has already allocated the required percentage (1 percent) of Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) in the 2018 Annual Budget amounting to P872,637.56.

observations.recommendation:

utilize fully the 20% DF to ensure that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that may be derived by observing the timeliness of activities involved in the procurement process thru proper planning to ensure that projects programmed for the year are implemented; and

instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the  Office of the Municipal Engineer to fast tract the implementation of  infrastructure projects funded  from the 20% DF, as well as projects  funded by National Government Agencies for the benefits of the constituents.

No action was undertaken in CY 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to update the Property Cards and the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards to resolve the difference in the PPE accounts. Likewise, continuous coordination between said officials should be made in order to secure relevant documents that will clarify, substantiate, and establish information leading to the completeness of the inventory reports and reconciliation of the differences; and

exert all efforts to retrieve records, information from concerned officials and employees, through inquiry, inspection and confirmation in order  to determine whether the  PPE  are serviceable or unserviceable  and  initiate the disposal of the same in accordance with  the above cited procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to  revert the amount of the outstanding payable accounts to the unappropriated surplus to be available for funding other vital projects of the  Municipality pursuant to Section 98 of P.D. The Municipal Accountant and  Municipal Treasurer ensured that all payments are made by checks.


The BAC used the alternative mode of procurement in the purchase of goods and services.

Mobile cellular consumptions were within the allowable cellular load allowance.

Use of mobile cellular with free calls was adopted.


With control measures adopted.



Use of mobile cellular under post paid plan.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended the Municipal Mayor to  require the Municipal Budget Officer  in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to  revert the amount of P708,604.37 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund to be available for other social services.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made by the Municipal Accountant for the reversion of P708,604.37 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to enforce collection of the  remaining balance of  P53,215.00 which could be used for its  programs and projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Efforts had been exerted by the Municipal Treasurer by sending demand letters to the defaulted stall holders who are still residing in the Municipality.  As of year-end, promissory notes were executed by the stallholders promising to pay their dues.

observations.recommendation: We  reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor utilize fully the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that may be derived from the projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts by sending demand letters to the vendors of the old public market to enforce collection of the delinquencies totaling P53,215.00 which could be used for its  projects and programs of the Municipality.

The Municipal Treasurer failed to collect the whole amount of market fees since some of the vendors from whom the amounts are due are no longer existing (some are deceased already).
